# Epilepsy Patient Population — FHIR Query Set

> **Strategy:** Three parallel query groups (Diagnoses → Medications → EEG). Each query uses `_include=:patient` so Patient resources are returned alongside the matching resources, enabling direct union by Patient ID. The `:below` modifier expands codes to all descendants via the server's terminology service (ICD-10, SNOMED, RxNorm hierarchies must be loaded).

---

## 🔷 GROUP 1 — Epilepsy Diagnoses (`Condition`)

| # | Target | Code System | Code | Coverage |
|---|--------|-------------|------|----------|
| 1a | Epilepsy & recurrent seizures | ICD-10-CM | G40 | All G40.x subtypes via `:below` |
| 1b | Status epilepticus | ICD-10-CM | G41 | All G41.x subtypes via `:below` |
| 1c | Epilepsy disorder | SNOMED CT | 84757009 | All epilepsy subtypes via `:below` |
| 1d | Seizure disorder (parent) | SNOMED CT | 128613002 | Broader parent; captures variants |
| 1e | Status epilepticus | SNOMED CT | 230460007 | Explicit; may sit outside G84757009 tree |

```
Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|G40&_include=Condition:patient

Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|G41&_include=Condition:patient

Condition?code:below=http://snomed.info/sct|84757009&_include=Condition:patient

Condition?code:below=http://snomed.info/sct|128613002&_include=Condition:patient

Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|230460007&_include=Condition:patient
```

> ⚠️ **Optional filter:** Append `&clinical-status=active` to restrict to currently active diagnoses. Omitting it captures historical/resolved documentation, which is useful for population identification.

---

## 🔷 GROUP 2 — Anti-Epileptic Drug (AED) Prescriptions (`MedicationRequest` + `MedicationStatement`)

Both resource types are queried: `MedicationRequest` = prescriptions written; `MedicationStatement` = patient/provider-reported usage. The `:below` modifier on ingredient-level RxNorm codes captures all branded/generic formulations (tablets, extended-release, IV, suspension, etc.).

| Drug | RxNorm Ingredient Code |
|------|----------------------|
| Phenytoin | 8123 |
| Carbamazepine | 2002 |
| Levetiracetam | 229002 |
| Valproic acid / Valproate | 11118 |
| Lamotrigine | 28439 |

### MedicationRequest (prescriptions)
```
MedicationRequest?code:below=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|8123&_include=MedicationRequest:patient

MedicationRequest?code:below=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|2002&_include=MedicationRequest:patient

MedicationRequest?code:below=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|229002&_include=MedicationRequest:patient

MedicationRequest?code:below=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|11118&_include=MedicationRequest:patient

MedicationRequest?code:below=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|28439&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

### MedicationStatement (reported/reconciled medications)
```
MedicationStatement?code:below=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|8123&_include=MedicationStatement:patient

MedicationStatement?code:below=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|2002&_include=MedicationStatement:patient

MedicationStatement?code:below=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|229002&_include=MedicationStatement:patient

MedicationStatement?code:below=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|11118&_include=MedicationStatement:patient

MedicationStatement?code:below=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|28439&_include=MedicationStatement:patient
```

> ⚠️ **Status filter for adherence work:** Once the population is identified, scope active prescriptions with `&status=active,on-hold` on `MedicationRequest` queries.

---

## 🔷 GROUP 3 — EEG Studies (`Procedure` + `DiagnosticReport`)

EEG is stored in two resource types depending on the system: `Procedure` (the act of performing the EEG) and `DiagnosticReport` (the interpreted result). Both are queried with SNOMED and LOINC codes.

| # | Resource | Code System | Code | Description |
|---|----------|-------------|------|-------------|
| 3a | Procedure | SNOMED CT | 54550000 | Electroencephalogram (procedure) |
| 3b | Procedure | LOINC | 18107-6 | EEG study |
| 3c | DiagnosticReport | LOINC | 18107-6 | EEG study (interpreted report) |

```
Procedure?code=http://snomed.info/sct|54550000&_include=Procedure:patient

Procedure?code=http://loinc.org|18107-6&_include=Procedure:patient

DiagnosticReport?code=http://loinc.org|18107-6&_include=DiagnosticReport:patient
```

---

## 📊 Query Summary

| Group | Resource Types | Queries | Codes Used |
|-------|---------------|---------|------------|
| Epilepsy Diagnoses | `Condition` | 5 | ICD-10-CM G40/G41, SNOMED 84757009/128613002/230460007 |
| AED Prescriptions | `MedicationRequest`, `MedicationStatement` | 10 | RxNorm 8123/2002/229002/11118/28439 |
| EEG Studies | `Procedure`, `DiagnosticReport` | 3 | SNOMED 54550000, LOINC 18107-6 |
| **Total** | **4 resource types** | **18** | |

---

## 🔗 Building the Union — Post-Query Steps

```
1. Collect all returned resources from all 18 queries
2. Extract Patient references:
   - Condition.subject
   - MedicationRequest.subject
   - MedicationStatement.subject
   - Procedure.subject
   - DiagnosticReport.subject
   OR use the _include=:patient bundles directly
3. Deduplicate by Patient.id → this is your epilepsy population
4. Tag each patient with which signal(s) they matched
   (Dx only | AED only | EEG only | combinations)
   for downstream QI stratification
```

> **Note on `:below` support:** If your FHIR server does not support the `:below` modifier, replace each `:below` query with individual `code=system|code1,system|code2,...` queries listing each specific subcode (e.g., `G40.001,G40.009,G40.011,...`). This is also advisable as a fallback validation step.

```

## 🗂️ Query Summary & Deduplication Strategy

| # | Query | Resource Type | Cohort Criteria |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1A | SNOMED epilepsy concepts | `Condition` | Dx — SNOMED |
| 1B | ICD-10 G40.x via `:below` | `Condition` | Dx — ICD-10 epilepsy |
| 1C | ICD-10 G41.x via `:below` | `Condition` | Dx — ICD-10 status epilepticus |
| 1D | ICD-10 explicit subcodes (fallback) | `Condition` | Dx — ICD-10 fallback |
| 2A | 5 AEDs, active prescriptions | `MedicationRequest` | AED prescribed |
| 2B | 5 AEDs, reported medications | `MedicationStatement` | AED patient-reported |
| 3A | EEG procedures performed | `Procedure` | EEG documented |
| 3B | EEG diagnostic reports | `DiagnosticReport` | EEG reported |

### Deduplication
Extract `Patient.id` from all included Patient resources across all 8 queries, then:
```
patients_union = set(patients_1A) | set(patients_1B) | set(patients_1C) | set(patients_1D)
               | set(patients_2A) | set(patients_2B)
               | set(patients_3A) | set(patients_3B)
```

> 🔎 **Tip for follow-up QI queries:** Once your patient list is established, run `MedicationRequest?subject=Patient/[id]&status=active` per patient to assess current AED regimen, and `Appointment?patient=[id]&status=booked,fulfilled&date=ge2025-06-18` to evaluate follow-up compliance.

### 🗺️ Code Selection Rationale

**Epilepsy Conditions — SNOMED (Query 1a):** 29 core clinical codes spanning the full epilepsy hierarchy: parent concept `84757009`, seizure disorder `128613002` (confirmed in server sample), major lobe-specific epilepsies, named syndromes (Lennox-Gastaut, Dravet, Landau-Kleffner, Rasmussen), generalized/focal/genetic epilepsies, and developmental epileptic encephalopathies. Returned 1,602 records.

**Epilepsy Conditions — ICD-10-CM (Query 1b):** Union of Mathematica + CSTE active value sets = 62 unique ICD-10-CM codes. Split into two batches for URL safety:
- **Batch A:** G40.001–G40.824 (focal, generalized, external-cause, Lennox-Gastaut, infantile spasm subtypes) — 413 records
- **Batch B:** G40.833–G40.C19 + G83.84 (Todd's paralysis) + G93.45 (developmental epileptic encephalopathy) + R56.1 (post-traumatic seizures) + R56.9 (unspecified convulsions — high-volume code confirmed in server sample) — 1,585 records. *Note: eclampsia O15.xx excluded (obstetric, not epilepsy QI population); serotonin syndrome G90.81 excluded.*

**AEDs — RxNorm (Query 2):** 25 RxNorm codes from Brigham Antiepileptics value set. Confirmed server uses RxNorm. All 5 user-specified AEDs verified in server sample. Full class included (levetiracetam `114477`, valproate `40254`, carbamazepine `2002` all seen directly). Extended to lacosamide, zonisamide, topiramate, oxcarbazepine, phenobarbital, gabapentin, clonazepam, perampanel, rufinamide, etc.

**EEG Procedures — SNOMED + CPT (Query 3):** VSAC EEG value set is **not used** — those 19 LOINC codes are quantitative ICU brain monitoring observations (burst suppression ratio, BIS index, etc.), not diagnostic EEG procedure records. Server Procedure sample confirmed SNOMED `54550000` (Electroencephalogram) + CPT `95816`. Full EEG CPT family added: 95816 (awake+drowsy), 95819 (awake+asleep), 95822 (sleep only), 95824 (comatose), 95827 (all night), 95829 (electrocorticography), 95830 (portable).

3-broad.prompt_text:
I need to identify my complete epilepsy patient population for a quality improvement initiative on AED adherence and follow-up. From clinical experience, epilepsy patients are documented in different ways: most have a formal epilepsy diagnosis (parent epilepsy, generalized, seizure disorder, status epilepticus), most are on AEDs (phenytoin, carbamazepine, levetiracetam, valproate, lamotrigine), and most have undergone EEG for diagnosis or surveillance.

Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:
1. Patients with any epilepsy diagnosis
2. Patients prescribed AEDs (phenytoin, carbamazepine, levetiracetam, valproate, lamotrigine)
3. Patients who have undergone EEG

For each query, return the matching resources. I want the union of all patients found.

## 🔬 Methodology Summary

|---|---|
| `Condition` | SNOMED CT + ICD-10-CM + ICD-9-CM (all three co-coded per record) |
| `MedicationRequest` | RxNorm |
| `Procedure` | SNOMED CT + CPT (both co-coded per record) |

| **3 — EEG Procedures** | `Procedure` | SNOMED 54550000 + 6 CPT EEG codes | **340** |

> **Note:** Resource counts ≠ unique patient counts. The union of *patients* across arms is your final cohort; many patients will appear in multiple arms (e.g., a patient with a G40.909 diagnosis, a levetiracetam prescription, and an EEG).

|---|---|

## FHIR Queries: Antiepileptic Drug (AED) Prescriptions by Drug Class

---

### 🔵 Class 1 — Sodium Channel Blockers (Phenytoin, Carbamazepine, Lamotrigine)

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|8134,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|2002,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|28439&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:patient&_include=MedicationRequest:medication
```

---

### 🟢 Class 2 — SV2A Modulators (Levetiracetam)

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|131725&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:patient&_include=MedicationRequest:medication
```

---

### 🟠 Class 3 — Broad-Spectrum AEDs (Valproate / Valproic Acid)

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|11118&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:patient&_include=MedicationRequest:medication
```

---

### 🔍 Combined Single Query (All AED Classes)

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|8134,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|2002,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|28439,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|131725,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|11118&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:patient&_include=MedicationRequest:medication
```

---

### 📋 RxNorm Code Reference

| Drug | RxNorm Code | Drug Class |
|------|-------------|------------|
| Phenytoin | `8134` | Sodium Channel Blocker |
| Carbamazepine | `2002` | Sodium Channel Blocker |
| Lamotrigine | `28439` | Sodium Channel Blocker |
| Levetiracetam | `131725` | SV2A Modulator |
| Valproic Acid | `11118` | Broad-Spectrum AED |

> **Notes:**
> - `status=active` filters for current prescriptions; change to `status=active,completed` to include historical prescriptions
> - `_include=MedicationRequest:patient` pulls associated Patient resources in one bundle
> - `_include=MedicationRequest:medication` resolves external Medication references when used
> - Comma-separated codes apply **OR** logic within the `code` parameter

1-expert.raw_response:
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|8183,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|2002,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|114477,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|40254,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|28439
```

---

### Query Breakdown

| RxNorm Code | Drug | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `8183` | Phenytoin | Classic AED; narrow therapeutic index |
| `2002` | Carbamazepine | AED + trigeminal neuralgia + bipolar |
| `114477` | Levetiracetam | Most-prescribed modern AED; broad-spectrum |
| `40254` | Valproate / Valproic acid | AED + bipolar + migraine prophylaxis |
| `28439` | Lamotrigine | AED + bipolar maintenance |

### Key Design Decisions

- **Single query, comma-separated values** — In FHIR, comma separation within a single parameter represents **OR** logic, satisfying all five drugs in one round-trip.
- **`code` parameter** — Targets `MedicationRequest.medication.ofType(CodeableConcept)` (inline coded medications). If your server stores medications as external `Medication` references, you may need to chain: `MedicationRequest?medication.code=...` or use `_include=MedicationRequest:medication` alongside a `Medication?code=...` query.
- **Ingredient-level RxNorm codes** — These are **ingredient** (IN) codes, so they match across all dose forms, strengths, and brand names (e.g., Keppra → levetiracetam `114477`). If your server indexes **clinical drug** (SCD/SBD) codes instead of ingredients, you may need to expand to product-level RxNorm codes.
- **Cross-indication awareness** — Results for `2002`, `40254`, and `28439` may include non-epilepsy prescriptions; correlate with `reasonCode` or linked `Condition` resources if epilepsy-specific filtering is needed.

1-naive.raw_response:
EEGs (Electroencephalograms) can appear across multiple FHIR resources. Here are the relevant queries:

**DiagnosticReport** (EEG reports):
```
DiagnosticReport?code=http://loinc.org|18107-2&_include=DiagnosticReport:patient&_include=DiagnosticReport:subject
```

**Procedure** (EEG procedures performed):
```
Procedure?code=http://snomed.info/sct|54550000&_include=Procedure:patient&_include=Procedure:subject
```

**Observation** (EEG findings/results):
```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|18107-2&_include=Observation:patient&_include=Observation:subject
```

---

### Notes:
| Resource | Code System | Code | Display |
|---|---|---|---|
| DiagnosticReport / Observation | LOINC | `18107-2` | Electroencephalogram (EEG) Study |
| Procedure | SNOMED CT | `54550000` | Electroencephalogram (procedure) |

- **`_include=...:patient`** and **`_include=...:subject`** are included to retrieve the associated `Patient` resources in the bundle.
- If your system uses ICD-10-PCS or CPT codes internally, additional queries with those code systems may be needed.
- Consider also querying `ServiceRequest?code=...` if you want to find **ordered** (but not yet completed) EEGs.

```

---

### Query Breakdown

| Query | System | Code | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Procedure?code=http://snomed.info/sct\|54550000` | SNOMED CT | 54550000 | Electroencephalogram (procedure) |
| `Procedure?code=http://loinc.org\|19954-3` | LOINC | 19954-3 | Electroencephalogram study |
| `Procedure?code=...cpt\|95816` | CPT | 95816 | EEG, routine (awake & drowsy) |
| `Procedure?code=...cpt\|95819` | CPT | 95819 | EEG, extended (awake & asleep) |

### Notes
- **SNOMED CT `54550000`** is the primary semantic concept and most interoperable choice across FHIR systems.
- **LOINC `19954-3`** is commonly used in diagnostic report and observation contexts but may appear on Procedure records in some implementations.
- **CPT codes (95816, 95819)** are widely used in US clinical billing systems; both cover routine and extended EEG variants respectively.
- If your server supports `_include`, you can augment queries to pull related DiagnosticReport resources: `Procedure?code=http://snomed.info/sct|54550000&_include=Procedure:subject`

1-expert.raw_response:
```
Procedure?code=http://snomed.info/sct|54550000
```

**Query Breakdown:**

| Component | Value | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| **Resource** | `Procedure` | Targets performed/documented procedures, not diagnoses or medications |
| **`code`** | `http://snomed.info/sct\|54550000` | SNOMED CT canonical code for *Electroencephalogram (procedure)* |

---

**Clinical Context:**
- **SNOMED CT `54550000`** — *Electroencephalogram (procedure)* is the standard, non-invasive neurophysiologic study used to evaluate electrical brain activity in epilepsy workup
- Complements the **diagnosis path** (`Condition` with ICD-10 G40.x epilepsy codes) and the **medication path** (`MedicationRequest` with antiepileptic drugs like valproate, levetiracetam, etc.)
- This procedural evidence captures patients who underwent diagnostic evaluation even if an epilepsy diagnosis or prescription isn't yet documented

---

**Recommended Enhancements:**

To broaden coverage or include patient context, consider:

```
# Include related patient demographics
Procedure?code=http://snomed.info/sct|54550000&_include=Procedure:patient

# Restrict to completed EEGs only
Procedure?code=http://snomed.info/sct|54550000&status=completed

# EEGs performed in the last 2 years
Procedure?code=http://snomed.info/sct|54550000&date=ge2024-06-18

# Patients who have BOTH an EEG procedure AND an epilepsy condition
Procedure?code=http://snomed.info/sct|54550000&_has:Condition:patient:code=http://snomed.info/sct|84757009
```

> ⚠️ Some systems may also use **LOINC `55712-9`** (*EEG study*) or local codes — consider a ValueSet-based search (`code:in`) if cross-terminology coverage is needed.
